_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-11 SPRN Patient Containment Algorithm (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-11: SPRN Patient Containment Algorithm — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define the three SPRN patient classification types and corresponding containment/transport requirements for Special Pathogen patients.

sections.title: SPRN Patient Classification Overview
content: SPRN patients are classified into THREE types based on exposure history and symptom status:
— TYPE 1: Person Under Investigation (PUI)
— TYPE 2: Low Risk / Dry
— TYPE 3: High Risk / Wet
Classification determines containment level, PPE requirements, and transport procedures
Classification is determined by MDHHS/SPRN coordinator and public health — EMS receives classification before or upon patient contact

sections.title: Type 1 — Person Under Investigation (PUI)
content: Definition: individual with possible exposure to HCID and symptoms meeting PUI criteria, but not yet confirmed as SPRN case
Examples: recent travel from outbreak region with fever; healthcare worker with potential exposure to confirmed HCID case
PPE requirement: standard SPRN PPE (PAPR or full-face respirator + fluid-resistant coveralls + gloves + boot covers)
Containment: standard SPRN transport precautions; patient isolation in ambulance
Transport: to SPRN-capable facility; no isolation device required for PUI unless patient is 'wet'
Coordinate with MDHHS before transport — do NOT self-dispatch PUI to hospital without coordination

sections.title: Type 2 — Low Risk / Dry
content: Definition: patient with known or confirmed HCID exposure but WITHOUT active bleeding, vomiting, diarrhea, or excessive secretions (dry patient)
Containment: no specialized isolation transport device required
PPE requirement: full SPRN PPE (PAPR or full-face respirator + fluid-resistant coveralls + gloves + boot covers)
Transport: standard SPRN transport precautions; EMS crew uses full SPRN PPE; patient wears surgical mask
No Medical Isolation Transport Device (MITD) required for dry patient
Vehicle cleaning per Protocol 10-15 after transport

sections.title: Type 3 — High Risk / Wet
content: Definition: patient with confirmed or highly suspected HCID WITH active or recent bleeding, vomiting, diarrhea, excessive secretions, or body fluid exposure risk (wet patient)
Containment: REQUIRES Medical Isolation Transport Device (MITD) — vinyl enclosed negative air pressure containment unit (Protocol 10-16)
PPE requirement: maximum SPRN PPE (PAPR + full-face respirator + fluid-resistant coveralls + double gloves + boot covers + head cover)
Transport: SPRN transport procedures per Protocols 10-13 and 10-14; MITD assembled and patient placed before transport
Two-team handoff required at receiving facility (Protocol 10-16)
Death during transport: Protocol 10-18 applies — no interventions; notify CHECC

sections.title: Classification Algorithm
content: STEP 1: Is patient a confirmed or suspected HCID case?
— YES → proceed to Step 2
— NO → standard protocols; consider Protocol 10-9 (AFRI) if pandemic suspected
STEP 2: Does patient have active symptoms (fever, malaise)?
— NO → not currently a SPRN transport indication; monitor; re-evaluate
— YES → proceed to Step 3
STEP 3: Is confirmation status PUI or confirmed HCID?
— PUI (unconfirmed) → TYPE 1: SPRN transport with full PPE; no MITD required
— Confirmed HCID → proceed to Step 4
STEP 4: Does patient have active bleeding, vomiting, diarrhea, or significant secretions?
— NO (dry) → TYPE 2: SPRN transport with full PPE; no MITD
— YES (wet) → TYPE 3: SPRN transport with full PPE + MITD

sections.title: Inter-Facility SPRN Transport
content: Inter-facility (hospital to hospital) SPRN transport follows the same containment algorithm
Sending hospital must provide classification (Type 1/2/3) to SPRN transport team before patient transfer
MITD assembled at sending hospital if Type 3 transport
All cross-MCA transport rules apply per Protocol 10-10

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: Type 2 (Dry) patient develops active bleeding, vomiting, or diarrhea during transport — reclassify as Type 3 (Wet); initiate MITD protocol if available; contact SPRN coordinator
Unable to confirm patient classification before transport — treat as highest risk (Type 3) until confirmed otherwise
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. Patient type classification (PUI, Low Risk/Dry, High Risk/Wet) drives the containment and transport decisions. Type 3 (Wet) requires the Medical Isolation Transport Device (MITD) — see Protocol 10-16. Classification is provided by MDHHS/SPRN coordinator, not independently determined by EMS.
